assumptions: The dividend receivable is a recognised intragroup monetary item denominated in a currency that creates foreign exchange differences in the consolidated financial statements.
The stated foreign exchange exposure in consolidation means the related exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation and could affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Foreign currency hedge of an intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 provides a specific route to designate the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary receivable or payable as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements when the related exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
